# Rally

## Mental model

Rally is a Gleam package for building Lustre apps that render on the server and hydrate in the browser. You write page modules and `server_*` handler functions. Rally generates routing, server-side rendering, WebSocket transport, and typed client-server messaging.

The page file is the contract. Client state, server calls, and the message types that cross the wire live together until you extract shared code. Libero scans server handler signatures, walks the type graph, and generates the wire protocol. Rally handles pages, routing, SSR, transport, and runtime. Libero handles the typed RPC contract, encode/decode, request envelopes, and response frames.

Rally apps use SQLite by default: embedded database, migrations, and type-safe SQL codegen, with no separate database server for development.

ETF is the default wire format. JSON is available for non-Gleam clients.

## Quick start

```sh
gleam new my_app
cd my_app
gleam add rally libero
gleam run -m rally init
./bin/dev
```

`rally init` creates the starter app files, including `bin/dev`. After that, `./bin/dev` runs Rally codegen (scans `src/<namespace>/pages/`, generates namespaced router, dispatch, RPC dispatch, SSR handler, WS handler, HTTP handler, and client package), optionally runs Marmot for SQL codegen, builds the JS client, and starts the server on port 8080.

`APP_ENV=dev` is the default. Set `APP_ENV=prod` in production for secure session cookies and quieter console output.

## Page conventions

A page file in `src/<namespace>/pages/` maps to a URL route. The filename determines the route:

| File | URL | Route variant |
|---|---|---|
| `home_.gleam` or `index.gleam` | `/` | `Home` |
| `about.gleam` | `/about` | `About` |
| `products/id_.gleam` | `/products/:id` | `ProductsId(id: Int)` |

Trailing underscore marks a dynamic segment. Names ending in `_id` become `Int` params; everything else `String`.

### Required exports

```gleam
pub type Model { ... }
pub type Msg { ... }
pub fn init() -> #(Model, Effect(Msg))
pub fn update(model: Model, msg: Msg) -> #(Model, Effect(Msg))
pub fn view(model: Model) -> Element(Msg)
```

Apps with `client_context.gleam` receive `ClientContext` as the first argument to `init`, `update`, and `view`.

### SSR loading

Pages can export `load` (server-side data fetch before first render) and `init_loaded` (client init from pre-fetched data). The model is base64-ETF encoded and embedded as `window.__RALLY_FLAGS__`.

### Layouts

`layout.gleam` provides page chrome. Rally assigns the nearest layout above a page route.

## Server messaging

### RPC (stateless, recommended default)

Define a message type and a `server_*` handler:

```gleam
pub type ServerLoadData { ServerLoadData(id: Int) }
pub fn server_load_data(
  msg msg: ServerLoadData,
  server_context server_context: ServerContext,
) -> Result(Data, List(String))
```

Client calls: `effect.rpc(ServerLoadData(id: 42), on_response: GotData)`.

Libero treats the handler signature as the wire contract source of truth.

### Stateful (bidirectional messaging)

Define `ToServer`/`ToClient` message types, a `ServerModel`, and `server_init`/`server_update`:

```gleam
pub type ToServer { ToggleFavorite; AddComment(body: String) }
pub type ToClient { ArticleUpdated(Article); CommentAdded(Comment) }
pub type ServerModel { ServerModel(article_id: Int) }
pub fn server_init(slug: String, server_context: ServerContext) -> #(ServerModel, Effect(ToClient))
pub fn server_update(model: ServerModel, msg: ToServer, server_context: ServerContext) -> #(ServerModel, Effect(ToClient))
```

The client sends `ToServer` messages with `effect.send_to_server`. Use stateful when the server needs state between calls (entity ownership for authorization) or server-initiated push.

### Broadcast

| Effect | Who receives it |
|---|---|
| `send_to_client(msg)` | One specific connection |
| `broadcast_to_session(msg)` | Every tab in the same browser session |
| `broadcast_to_page(msg)` | Every connection viewing the same page |
| `broadcast_to_app(msg)` | Every connection to the app |

Broadcast uses OTP pg process groups. Connections auto-subscribe on WebSocket connect.

### Effect primitives

From `rally_runtime/effect`:

- `rpc(msg, on_response: callback)`: stateless server call via WebSocket
- `send_to_server(msg)`: stateful ToServer message
- `send_to_client(variant)`: server push
- `broadcast_to_page(variant)` / `broadcast_to_app(variant)` / `broadcast_to_session(variant)`: multi-cast push
- `send_to_client_context(variant)`: cross-page state messaging
- `navigate(path)`: client-side URL push via modem
- `get_ws_session()`: session ID for current connection (server-side only)
- `read_lang()`: language cookie, then browser language, then `"en"`
- `effect.none()` / `effect.from(fn)`: standard Lustre effects

## Runtime modules

Most Rally apps import only a few `rally_runtime/*` modules:

| Module | Use it for |
|---|---|
| `rally_runtime/effect` | Page effects: RPC, server messages, navigation, broadcast, client context updates |
| `rally_runtime/db` | SQLite open, timed queries, nested transactions, SQL value helpers |
| `rally_runtime/system` | App startup, message logging, background jobs |
| `rally_runtime/session` | Session cookie generation, parsing, response headers |
| `rally_runtime/auth` | Auth policy and load result types |
| `rally_runtime/env` | `APP_ENV` parsing, production cookie policy |
| `rally_runtime/migrate` | Numbered SQLite migrations |
| `rally_runtime/test_db` | Fast in-memory SQLite for tests |
| `rally_runtime/topics` | OTP pg pub/sub for broadcast |
| `rally_runtime/wire` | Thin wrapper over libero wire helpers |
| `rally_runtime/codec` | Base64 ETF encode/decode for SSR flags |
| `rally_runtime/ssr` | Lustre element to HTML string |
| `rally_runtime/jobs` | Durable background job runner with retry |

## Configuration

```toml
[[tools.rally.clients]]
namespace = "public"
route_root = "/"
protocol = "etf"     # "etf" (default) or "json"
```

Multiple `[[tools.rally.clients]]` entries are supported, each with its own namespace, route root, and protocol. The codegen runs once per entry.

### Context types

**ServerContext** (`server_context.gleam`): server-side infrastructure. Rally generates `load(server_context)` calls; put DB connections and config here. Rally treats it as opaque.

**ClientContext** (`<namespace>/client_context.gleam`): cross-page client state. Shared by SSR and the browser SPA. Convention fields named `current_path`, `dark_mode`, or `lang` are synced from browser state at SPA boot.

**`from_session`**: when `<namespace>/client_context_server.gleam` exports `pub fn from_session(server_context:, session_id:, hostname:)`, Rally's SSR and HTTP handlers call it to build `#(ClientContext, ServerContext)`.

## Generated files

Running `gleam run -m rally` produces:

**Server-side** (`src/generated/<namespace>/`):

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `router.gleam` | Route type, `parse_route`, `route_to_path`, `href` |
| `page_dispatch.gleam` | PageModel/PageMsg unions, per-route init/update/view dispatch |
| `rpc_dispatch.gleam` | Routes wire messages to `server_*` handlers |
| `ssr_handler.gleam` | Calls `load`, renders `view` in layout, embeds model as flags |
| `ws_handler.gleam` | WebSocket frame loop: page topics, RPC dispatch, push delivery |
| `http_handler.gleam` | HTTP POST /rpc handler for non-WebSocket clients |
| `protocol_wire.gleam` | Protocol facade: delegates to libero ETF or JSON wire module |

**Client-side** (`.generated_clients/<namespace>/`):

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/generated/app.gleam` | Lustre SPA entry: per-page TEA loop, WebSocket transport, modem routing |
| `src/generated/transport.gleam` | FFI bridge to WebSocket runtime |
| `src/generated/types.gleam` | ClientMsg type mirroring server dispatch variants |
| `src/generated/codec.gleam` | SSR flag decoding for hydration |
| `src/<namespace>/pages/*.gleam` | Tree-shaken page modules (server code stripped) |
| `src/rally_runtime/effect.gleam` | Client-side effect shim |

The client package is a standalone Gleam project. The server project is the input to codegen.

## Internals

The `rally/internal/...` modules are Rally's codegen implementation. App code should treat them as private.

### Pipeline

```text
gleam.toml config -> scanner -> parser -> Libero handler scan -> generators -> tree shaker -> dependency resolver -> file output
```

### Codegen modules (`src/rally/internal/`)

| Module | Role |
|---|---|
| `rally.gleam` | CLI entry point, reads `[[tools.rally.clients]]`, runs one pipeline per client |
| `internal/scanner.gleam` | Walks pages directory, produces `List(ScannedRoute)` |
| `internal/parser.gleam` | Glance AST: extracts Model/Msg types, function signatures |
| `internal/generator.gleam` | Route type + parse_route + page dispatch codegen |
| `internal/generator/client.gleam` | Client package: gleam.toml, app.gleam, transport |
| `internal/generator/codec.gleam` | Client codegen: types, decoders, effect shim, per-page modules |
| `internal/generator/ssr_handler.gleam` | SSR handler generation |
| `internal/generator/ws_handler.gleam` | WebSocket handler generation |
| `internal/generator/http_handler.gleam` | HTTP RPC handler generation |
| `internal/generator/json_rpc_dispatch.gleam` | JSON-specific RPC dispatch |
| `internal/dependency_resolver.gleam` | Follows imports to copy shared modules, catches @external(erlang) |
| `internal/types.gleam` | Shared pipeline types: ScannedRoute, PageContract, ScanConfig |
| `internal/tree_shaker.gleam` | Strips server code from page source using Glance AST |
| `internal/format.gleam` | Runs `gleam format` on generated code |

Libero provides: scanner (server_ handler discovery), codegen_dispatch, walker (BFS type graph), codegen_decoders (JS type registration), field_type, glance_type_resolver (shared Glance type resolution), wire_identity (canonical type hashing), wire/protocol helpers, and trace (panic catching).

### Auth framework

Auth activates when `src/<namespace>/auth.gleam` exists. Required exports:

| Export | Signature | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `Identity` | type | App-defined identity type |
| `resolve` | `fn(ServerContext, String) -> Result(Identity, Nil)` | Session to identity |
| `is_authenticated` | `fn(Identity) -> Bool` | Whether identity is logged in |
| `redirect_url` | `String` | Where to redirect unauthenticated users |

Pages declare auth via `pub const page_auth = auth.Required` or `auth.Optional` after importing `rally_runtime/auth`. Pages can also export `pub fn authorize(server_context, identity) -> Bool`.

Auth-enabled pages return `LoadResult(data)` instead of bare `data`, supporting `Page(data, cookies)` and `Redirect(url, cookies)` variants. Types live in `rally_runtime/auth.gleam`.

Identity is excluded from the client-facing contract. Rally tells Libero's scanner to strip Identity params before message-type resolution. Server handlers still receive `identity: auth.Identity` as their last argument.

### Transport

WebSocket with auto-reconnect. HTTP POST /rpc for non-WebSocket clients. SSR renders full HTML with embedded flags on first request; the client reads flags and boots as a SPA.

### Dev tools

Message inspector enabled in `APP_ENV=dev`. Browser console shows `->` (client to server, orange), `<-` (server response, blue), `<<` (server push, purple). RPC responses include round-trip latency. `window.__RALLY_MESSAGES__` holds the full message history.

## Verification

```sh
gleam test
gleam format --check
gleam docs build
```

Integration check with the curling/v3 app:

```sh
cd /Users/daverapin/projects/curling/v3
bin/regen
bin/dev
```

- [Gleam](https://gleam.run)
- [Lustre](https://hexdocs.pm/lustre)
- [Libero](https://hexdocs.pm/libero)
- [Marmot](https://hexdocs.pm/marmot)
